message:
fix(codegen): Compile packages with Python source instead of C extension fallback

When encountering installed packages like numpy, check if the module
can be resolved to Python source before marking as C extension. This
allows packages with both .py and .so files to be compiled to Zig.

Changes:
- imports.zig: Add can_resolve_source check before C extension path
- imports.zig: Check source instead of .zig file for root modules
  (prevents timing issues during codegen)
- import_scanner.zig: Remove isCExtension skip to prefer Python source

Result: 9/11 numpy tests now pass (up from 0/11)

Remaining issues:
- test_ctypeslib: Needs numpy._core submodule (relative import handling)
- test_reloading: Needs numpy.exceptions (also relative import)
